body.title: Review + compact ALL Claude memories (MEMORY.md index + every topic file) AND CLAUDE.md
body.intention: MEMORY.md stays well under the read limit so the memory index always loads at session start (an over-limit index silently stops loading — directly worsening the forgetfulness it exists to fight).
body.description:
MEMORY.md is ~20KB against a 24.4KB read limit (PostToolUse hook flagged it 2026-06-26). Compact to under ~17KB: one line per entry, merge/drop genuinely stale or superseded entries (e.g. duplicate /sorted-vs-/decided naming notes, retired-goal entries once goal purge is fully done), shorten the longest hook descriptions, and push any over-long detail into the topic files. Must NOT drop a live, distinct fact — review each candidate against its topic file before merging/removing (see [[feedback_delete_after_successful_merge]]). Careful judgment task, not a blind trim.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/projects/-home-akien-dev-src-UnseenUniversity/memory/MEMORY.md (+ topic files if detail moves)
**Design rules:** none apply (CC auto-memory upkeep; not repo code).
**Scope boundary:** IN — shrink the index under the limit without losing distinct facts. OUT — rewriting topic-file bodies wholesale; auditing every memory for correctness (separate).
**Test plan:** After: `wc -c MEMORY.md` < 17400; spot-check that every pre-compaction topic file still has an index line (or was intentionally merged with a note); no live fact lost.
**Completion criteria:** MEMORY.md under ~17KB, one line per surviving entry, no distinct fact dropped, reviewed with Akien if any entry is removed rather than merged.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

---
## Scope EXPANDED 2026-07-10 (Akien): from MEMORY.md-only to ALL Claude memories + CLAUDE.md

Akien: "add all claude memories to T-compact-memory-index including CLAUDE.md." This is now a full
review-and-compact of the entire Claude memory surface, not just the index. Sibling of the skills
review ([[T-skills-review-prune-rearrange-intention-compat]]) — separate but related; same
"prune the accreted substrate to what earns its place" move, applied to memories + the bootstrap.

Three targets, one pass:
1. MEMORY.md index — get it under ~17KB (originally-scoped): one line/entry, merge/drop stale, push
   detail into topic files. (The PostToolUse hook now fires at ~19.6KB — genuinely near the ceiling.)
2. Every topic memory file (~/.claude/.../memory/*.md) — review each for: still-true? superseded?
   duplicate of another? stale file:line/flag that no longer exists (verify against current code
   before trusting — memories are point-in-time)? Merge/retire per [[feedback_delete_after_successful_merge]].
   Recent examples of the class: the open_questions-workflow memory was just superseded (folded into
   triage); watch for others carrying retired mechanisms (IMAP, palace DB, goals, /decided, IGOR_HOME).
3. CLAUDE.md — review the bootstrap for stale content: known-stale bits include the IMAP mentions in
   constraint text (IMAP is removed), the phase map, the core_values shim drift, and any rule whose
   "why" no longer matches the live substrate. Reconcile to current reality; keep the CP1-CP6 contract.

Intention-compat angle (ties to the skills review + workflow-levels): as memories/CLAUDE.md are
reviewed, flag anything that assumes the old goal/decision-era flow vs the intention-based workflow.

**Careful judgment task, not a blind trim** — never drop a live, distinct fact; verify each candidate
against its topic file / current code before merging or removing.
